review_text: Clean and comfortable cabin.

A lovely peaceful park with lots of amenities including kitchen, bbq, bouncy pillow thing, playground and communal fire  pit.  Sites are large and drive thru.  Power, water and grey water hook up.  Dump point near toilet block. Beautiful sunrise over misty fields with cattle grazing.
Funny and the people are good vibes and amazing service!

Wowwww... actually, I'm not at all into camping but my family brought me here and I have to admit, your camping site beats it all: super clean bathrooms, wonderful area with the animals - kangaroo, camel, the sweet pigs and ducks, the cat - really nice vibe with lots of space for everybody.
Amazing for kids since it comes with a huge trampoline.The kitchen area is as perfectly maintained as the whole camp site. Kudos to the team here 👏👏👏.
This is a great place to stay. Although it’s out of town, it’s close to a couple of lovely tourist attractions such as the Margaret River Chocolate Co & some fabulous wineries. Well worth a visit.
This is 20 km out of Margaret River in the middle of nowhere.  Got sucked in by the name and booked it.

Such a peaceful and quiet place—you can really feel the beauty of farm life. We celebrated my son’s birthday here while camping, and it turned out to be such a special experience. He absolutely loved feeding the animals and hasn’t stopped talking about it since. Now he keeps asking when we can come back again. Definitely a memorable and family-friendly getaway.
Beautiful place! Be sure to say hi to Guz at the office, he does not seem to get many visitors ☺️

We’ve been coming to this caravan park for over 30 years, long before Discovery took over — and honestly, what it’s become is a joke.

Since Discovery stepped in over the last couple of years, the place has gone downhill fast. The management is inconsistent, disorganised, and has zero regard for long-term customers. Last year we had multiple cars and different camping setups on our site and not a single word was said. This year, suddenly it’s an issue — with no prior warning and no consistency whatsoever. It’s ridiculous.

What makes it worse is how this “rule” is handled. We were told it’s in the terms and conditions, yet it was never mentioned last year, never shown to us, and isn’t clearly available or highlighted on their website when booking. The only time you’re actually presented with it is after you’ve already paid and are standing at the check-in desk being asked to sign. That’s not transparency — that’s misleading.

After decades of loyalty, you’d expect at least some level of respect and clear communication. Instead, it feels like a complete disregard for returning customers.

Safe to say we won’t be back. There are far better caravan parks in the area that are upfront, consistent, and actually know how to treat their guests.

Do yourself a favour and stay somewhere else.
Perfect location and enjoyed a good break

We stayed at Taunton Farm last night with my two young kids and unfortunately the experience wasn’t what we expected.

One of the main reasons we chose Taunton Farm was because it seemed like a quiet, family-friendly place to stay. However there was a large group staying at the property who were partying loudly, shouting and swearing until the early hours of the morning.

With young kids this made it very difficult to sleep and really took away from what we hoped would be a relaxing rural stay.

The property itself is lovely and the setting is great, but the lack of quiet hours or management of the noise meant the atmosphere felt far from family friendly.

Hopefully this is something the management can address in the future, as it has the potential to be a really great place.

Wunderschönen Platz direkt in der Natur mit sehr freundlichem Personal. Wir haben durch durch Glück noch spontan einen Platz bekommen und haben unseren kurzen Aufenthalt sehr genossen. Der Platz ist eher klein, die Stellplätze schön groß und beschattet. Es gibt umfassende Infrastruktur, ganze Badezimmer und eine volle ausgestattete, überdachte Küche. Und das alles zu einem fairen Preis. Da es sich um eine Farm handelt laufen Tiere frei herum, für Kinder gibt es auch einiges zu Entdecken. Einer der schönsten bezahlten Plätze in WA!

Campervan powered site
Lovely stop we stayed an extra night
Ok for price

My parents use to take my sister and I here every year, and now I'm taking my kids here. 30 years strong and no sign of stopping. This is our favourite caravan park, by far. I have so many fond memories, and I'm glad my kids will have the same experiences. The camp kitchen glow up is amazing. The bounce cushion is a hit with the kids of all ages. I hear they're talking about shade sails over playground which would be 10/10. Well done to the grounds crew. Park is immaculate. Reception staff are wonderful, warm and welcoming.

We had a great holiday here!

This caravan park was perfectly located for our 2 week South West trip. Max drive time to any of our sightseeing or activities was only 30mins.

We had a 2 bedroom, sleeps 4, cabin and it was perfect for what we needed. There were a few maintenance things that do need attention however, including the air conditioner (it struggled during the hot days) and the hot water system in the shower (it is extremely loud when in use).

The parks policy is while people are staying in the cabin, staff are not to enter it, which is great however with our stay being a lot longer than usual, it would have been nice to get an offer of our beds remade with fresh sheets half way through our stay. We were able to ask at any point for new towels, more toilet paper or complimentary tea/coffee/sugar sachets.

There was an awesome play area for kids, including a giant jumping pillow. We loved all the animal interactions with the ducks and brids; but especially loved the daily feeding of the resident pigs, cows and camel.

There was a huge camp kitchen with plenty of seating, four BBQs, two ovens, two stove tops, two microwaves, two fridges and three laundry sized sinks.

During our stay a lot of cars were not adhering to the 5km speed limit which is not safe with so many people, especially kids, walking and riding around the place. I'd really like to see more speed signs and some speed bumps installed throughout the park, especially up the hill towards the back where most of the cabins are.

The FREE school holiday activities were so good as well. Each day had an activity to do including; finding Maggie (the resident bulid-a-bear that you can win), guess the lollies in a jar (that you can also win) and a kids club for an hour where you could make your own stuffed teddy and do some colouring in on paper crowns and wooden key rings. Then for 2 hours each Sunday they even had a local company come and entertain the kids with face painting, balloon twisting, glitter tattoos and giant bubble blowing. Along with two different foods vans (pay for yourself) that came multiple times during our stay.

All the grounds maintenance and facilities were all well kept. The staff we interacted with were friendly and helpful. The new husband and wife management team are doing a great job.

All in all we had a fantastic time staying here. Keep up the great work. We will be back, hopefully very soon 🤞

Great place to stay
Not far from Margs or Busso
Great park area for the kids big grass area for them to play
Camp kitchen is massive
Kids can help feed the animals
Park managers were very good
We will be back
Sites are large
Great place to stay, very friendly and helpful. Worst internet and phone signal so don't go here if you need that. Other than that facilities were great.
Great location. Comfortable rooms. Lots of options for the kids to play and animal feeding was great fun!
Was really nice on the phone and respectful, the only weird thing was that when I put the address in Apple Maps it was in the wrong location
Highly recommended. We stayed at Cabin 11 and we personally liked it. Cabin is clean and spacious. Staffs are helpful and friendly specially the lovely lady at the reception.
Beautiful and peaceful place. Ideal for families or quick stop.  Definitely will come back
Loved our stay here! The staff were friendly, seamless after hours check in and the bathroom/kitchen facilities were very clean.
Great spot great facilties. Only dispapointmet was that it was a town away from Margaret River town. i.e. The otherside of Cowenup.
Awesome location, fantastic experience, kids love to feed the farm animals and the park itself is amazing. Friendly staff and peaceful setting not far from Margaret River.
Large cabin that was clean in campground conveniently located in Margaret River region

The best park we've stayed at! Animal Feeding, bouncy pillow, single bathrooms, kiosk, shaded grounds... and the views! A working cattle and sheep farm that's engaging, while still being relatively quiet. Great for kids I'd assume by the many happy families there. Great staff, like personal service and Mick's talks at the Feeding were educational and fun. The camp kitchens were borderline luxurious. Couldn't say a bad word about the place.

Great venue. Great location, great facilities, terrible management. Paid for a camp spot. 2 daya prior. Arrived in the day and checked in to park. Went oyt enjoyed my day. Only to have them book someone else in the site. Then they blamed me qouting if i had of taken a site map theyd have told me of the updated changed. The have a great play ground for kids. Absolute no management of curfew so kids swearing and riding around all night. Miles well sleep near a shop entry. Toilet relhm drain runs between the most kid friendly sites, so theres alot of bad smells close to the toilet block and play ground.
Nice place, good amenities, fun for the kids and spacious. Farm animals and rooster a bit too noisy for 4am. Other than that we thoroughly enjoyed ourselves
We loved to see the retired Animals and are this banjo frogs in the night? Amenities are clean and the ensuites are great.
Trying to book a space was an absolute Kafkaesque nightmare. All I wanted was to book a patch of dirt and the whole system was designed to make that impossible. Makes you want to scream.
Great place for families with kids, unfortunately the parents don't seem to be anywhere to be seen, making it not the idyllic place to camp and relax.

After nearly 14 months on the road, this has to be our favourite place to stay. Such beautiful grounds, animal feeding for the kids and the most wonderful staff. The owners of this park are wonderful people and so accommodating to make your stay happy and comfortable. Our kids have had a blast and don’t want to leave. Thank you again for an amazing stay.
Fantastic caravan park. Friendly staff, lovely location, grassy sites, lots of animals. Highly recommend.
Limited facilities but fantastic for young children

A long way out of any local town…. Which would be ok if it was quiet but lots of noise from main road at top of hill… speed 110…. You need to be cautious entering and leaving

Power and water could be a few sites over … so make sure you have a long leads and hose
Great location large powered sites, amenities spotless, well equipped kitchen, and friendly staff.
Great place to setup for a few days exploring

We have been to this camp site a couple of times and it’s become one of our favourites - great sized spots for our caravan and the kids love the facilities from the bouncing pillow to feeding the farm animals. On our latest visit there was the addition of crafts, sausage sizzle and a treasure hunt which the kids loved. The shower block was great and always clean.
The new owners have taken the camp establishment to a new level of service. Going out of their way to ensure that everyone is enjoying themselves.

Booked a powered site for 3 nights in Sep 2025. Grassy site next to the farm dam. Some areas of the park quite muddy due to winter rains. Ablution block closest to us had individual units but not all of them were unlocked. Clean enough but a bit tired. Friendly reception. Park good for families with a jumping pillow and daily feeding of farm animals. Some highway noise and the frogs in the dam were very noisy at night. Short drive to town.

Stayed for a few nights. Great location, great service and location was good. We were able to go to some touristy places with plenty of time to spare. Kids loved the play area and feeding the animals. Cinnamon 🐪 was our favourite. Facilities were great too.

At first we were a bit disappointed as it was not exactly in Margaret River, but 17 km before that. But then we realised how good is the place!
All facilities and playground are very close, kids took a ball on that jumping balloon, and animal feeding.
The place has everything you need, the even have shampoo, hair conditioner and soap dispenser in showers.
The kitchen even have pots! Wow!
Absolutely great place, and family friendly!
If I could give 6 stars I would!
Good park. Great for families.
Fantastic service,best amenity block after K11 of travelling. Keep up the great work
Not in Margaret River. Not within walking distance to anywhere.
Il posto preferito dei miei figli, possibilità di poter fare il BBQ…non volevamo venire via.

Amazing midweek stay. Cannot fault the chalet and service. Well stocked and clean chalet. The staff are amazing. Very friendly with an eye for detail. We received a call the day we were arriving asking what time we were expecting to arrive. We were amazed that when we arrived on a blustery, wet and cold day, the heater was on in the chalet. Amazing place. We will definitely be back.

Nice park. Great for families having a playground and animals.
Great camp kitchen
Unfortunately we had alot of rain and with only 1 slab in the park it was a soggy stay.
Staff are friendly
Stayed here in our van. Place is amazing, staff are exceptionally welcoming and polite. Kids loved the animals. Will definitely come back.

An amazing winter stay. Fire pits you can BYO or hire. We stayed in a drive through site opposite the playground, you would think it would be over the top loud but it wasn't at all.
Sites are huge which is absolutely amazing you don't feel like you're ontop of each other.

So much for the kids to do during the holidays
Staff were absolutely amazing. Super friendly people.

Will definitely be back again.
Highly recommend it
Amazing family orientated farm style caravan park lovely chalets and friendly staff, will be back again,

We chose to do the Ord River Discoverer with Sunset. We have done it before and thought after  5 years it would be a lovely way to spend the day.  Chris was our guide,  he was very professional and knew all about the Ord, Argyle and Kununurra.  He took time to find animals, birds and crocs for us to see and photograph, also reflections.........they were stunning.|Afternoon tea was provided by Sue..........she had cooked us Pumpkin Scones and apple/cinnamon muffins. This was setup by Chris and  welcomed by us all.|Phil dropped us home in the Triple J bus.|All the staff were upbeat and happy and imparted all sorts of information along the way.

This is fundamentally a good park.

Pros:
+ The sites (drive through powered) are of good size.
+ Staff are super friendly

Cons:
- Water pressure is very poor, be prepared to be underwhelmed.
- Location … The location is alright, however it’s still 16klms outside of Margaret River township.
- Very sandy sites, we spent more time trying to get sand out of our setup than we did relaxing!

Overall, I think it was a good park, just not sure it’s worth the $$$ for the cons.

This is an amazing park for kids and family’s comfy cozy accommodation. Lots to do in the area they hire out fires to have outside your cabin . Animal feeding is fantastic little ones are so into it .
We stayed in a chalet a great place to stay.

Our 2nd stop on our down south trip saw as at Discovery Parks Margaret River for 2 nights.

📍 Location & Check-in
🚗 Distance: 2hr 40min South of Perth CBD
📚 Check-in: Was very easy at the reception desk, given a park map and off me went. (Fully paid at time of booking)
🕐 In: 2pm for sites & cabins
⏰️ Out: 10am for both

🏕 Amenities Check
🛁 Toilets & Showers: Located in the middle of the park, always clean, not moden but very spacious, 4 toilets and showers and also a baby sink in the women's block. Shampoo, conditioner and body wash provided.
Water pressure and temperature wasn't great.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family Room: Yes a very large Family/Disable room with a change table. No bench space.
🍳 Camp Kitchen: Is a huge undercover open area with lots of seating. Has everything you need with a large communal firepit.
🪁Kids Fun Factor: older kids play ground (not suited to toddlers), jumping pillow, large grass area, free animal feeding at 3pm.

🍩 Coffee & Nearby Cafés
☕️ Onsite Coffee Van: Nope, caravan coffee only.
🧁 Closest Cafe/Food Spot: The office had a simple rage of dry goods, milk, drinks and ice cream.
🏭 Cowaramup Township (5 minute drive) amazing lollie shop, brewery, cafes, and small boutique shops and lots of statute cows (the kids loved) must visit such a beautiful town.
🏙Margaret River is another 10 minutes south and has Coles and Woolworths for supplies.

🏠 Accommodation
⛺️ Cabins, Powered and Non-Powered sites available
💲We paid $107 for 2 nights on a powered site for: 2 Adults + 1 Child (3-16) + 1 Infant (free)

🐾 Pet-Friendly
🐶 Dogs Allowed: On leash at all times.

🗣 Heads Up!
🚐 Site: We loved the size of the site.
🔥 Fires: Permitted in season.
At your site with your own firepit or at the communal pit at the camp kitchen
🚿 Showers: Water pressure sucked and the temperature fluctuated the whole time.
👩 Staff: Were friendly and always checked in if they walked past. Lady at the desk ask if I would like information or recommendations on activities, where to eat or what to do in the area. (first time since we started traveling I had been asked)

🚐 Would We Bring Reggie Back?
Absolutely / Yes / Maybe / No
✅ Yes! It's a beautiful spot, central to lots of things, Margaret River only be 10 minutes away, surrounded by breweries and wineries and so many things to see and do! We would stay longer next time!

Follow us on instagram @aadriannaconnor to see all the places we stay and review.

We stayed on a powered site, set amongst trees, right near the amenities and jumping pillow/playground. We stayed during Winter so could have our own firepit. Our outlook was beautiful - grassy clean paddocks dotted with various farm animals - cows, pigs, ducks and even a camel. My kids loved the animal feeding each day at 3.30pm. We enjoyed the peace & tranquility and its location is ideal - 15 minutes from Margaret River centre but close to the cute little village of Cowaramup and very central to all of the wineries, beaches & attractions. Loved ours stay here, highly recommend!
Good park friendly staff. Definitely worth staying at, good and clean.

Good caravan park to visit.
Large community camp area and playground for kids.

Animal feeding at 3:30pm for everyone to enjoy.

Free soap, shampoo in the shower blocks was a nice suprise.

Fire pits allowed at certain times of year which is a nice outdoor family activity.

Only downside of the park was the flooding of the sites was over the top when the rain hit. Some better drainage on sites would definitely be needed before I choose to camp on them again.

Parked the caravan in one of the powered sites for 4 nights. Even though some nights were fully booked it still felt like no one around. Decent playground and farm animal feeding at 3.30 each arvo. Looking forward to staying again. Staff really friendly and up for a yarn

Its a lovely caravan park . Nestled amongst the trees.  Heaps of things for the kids to do , including feeding the animals at 3:30.the only problem we had as when the cleaners were cleaning the toilet.  We had to go to the disabled toilet , it was a bit hard when someone was in there for 30 to 45 minutes .There are 6 more toilets down the back but they weren't open.

Great location. It's actually just outside Cowarumup so a little drive into Margaret River itself. But excellent coffee to be had in Cowarumup (try Two Cracks) to get you on your way. Also great cycling track from Cowarumup into Margaret River along what was once a rail track. Be ware though, it's 15km of gentle downhill grade most of the way TOWARDS Margaret River. So it's a long ride back without any downhill relief! If you want to do it with kids, I recommend you arrange a ride for the return trip (ie. north out of MR)! Also a couple of road crossings to be ware of if you have kids - but worth the while.
Wonderful experience

What a beautiful little spot. We were on campsite 14 which was right next to the toilets, could see the jumping pillow (which the kids loved) never seen so many kids playing together. Great view too.
What I loved the most was the free activities... Animal feeding and go karts. Only downside was the art and crafts were only on a Fri and Sun at 2pm but that's check in time and nothing on the Saturday which seems very odd. We stayed at the RAC and it was so off putting when you had to pay for all the things I mentioned.
They have a little church which sold good coffee for $3.
They had run out of Soap in the ladies. I told a lady on site and she had it dealt with asap. Everyone who worked on site was truely so lovely. I would definitely stay again.

Well maintained and great location.

Have stayed here many times.

Shame about the dairy farm closing its doors was one of the main reasons we chose this location ... still very central to many of the Margaret River attractions. Would recommend.

This is a hard one to review. The most friendliest staff I've encountered. But the sites were just dirt despite photos showing caravans on grass. So, with all the rain we ended up  with mud and a lot of washing.  AND then, there were no clothes lines to hang all the washing ( $6 a load for drying is expensive). I was given a small clothes rack to borrow and told customers had ripped down the clothes lines?? I was assured new lines going up Monday. Tuesday, as I left, there were still no clothes lines.
It's right in a jungle which is the MOST AMAZING part of this! Such a ✌️ peace.  Everything over here is wonderful.
This place is awesome.  The facilities were so good and clean.  Plenty of space and looks over beautiful farmland.  Dog friendly as well.
Fantastic holiday park. my children enjoy with the park facilities and activities especially the animal feeding. Keep up the good work. will certainly visit again.

We booked this late as we decided to extend our stay in Margaret River. We arrived just in time for the animal feeding at 16:30 pm. The site is spacious and peaceful and is a perfect holiday camp for families. We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin which was spacious and very well equipped with a variety of crockery, glassware, cookware and general kitchen utensils, even had an iron and board. The cabin and shared amenities were clean and in good condition. WiFi strength was also good.  We only stayed for 1 night and I would 100% recommend this to anyone who plans to visit Margaret River.
Lovely park with amazing camp kitchen
Absolutely a great park the views are beautiful! We will be back for sure.
It's a great place to base yourself to see and taste all the area has to offer. We had our caravan so I can't comment on the rooms.
Great caravan park, very clean amenities friendly stuff good play area for the children animal feeding every afternoon

Not normally one to make google reviews but this was an extremely disappointing stay.
A group camping next to us was extremely loud after 10pm.  I tried to call the after hours number and it rang out twice with no answer. I called the following day to request some compensation. I was offered $5 back from my $50 one night booking. After speaking to the manager, I was offered $10 back and the promise of them fixing their after hours phone. Quite disappointing compensation for what occurred. A $10 refund and the park fixing their phone is hardly beneficial to me. What a waste of money that hopefully ‘peaceful’ night away was.

100/10
It is super kid friendly, playground, animals  and open space for them. The bathrooms are a cubical with a shower, toilet and sink and it is just luxury 😍
Location wise, you are 15 mins from Margaret River. 5 minutes from Cow Town and 30 minutes from Bussleton.
Kitchen has a microwave, stove, sink, bbq, kettle and toaster.
Highly recommend this place!!!!!!

Lovely park. Well staffed and you feel very secure here.
Just up the road from an amazingly cute little town Cowaramup. Just beautiful bike rides and lovely scenery. If you enjoy exploring this is a great little find. I have a permanent, painful injury that is aided with riding as part of my ongoing rehab so these finds being me much joy.
Well run park. The sausage sizzle was unexpected and we are very thankful for it. The animal feeding was lots of fun and very engaging for everyone.
Thanks guys!
Great location. Has lots of farm animals on it, plus a camel. They do animal feeding every day at 430pm. Lots of shaded sites. Lots of mosquitoes and sand flies so be prepared. Ensuites are nice.

Love the animal feeding (daily at 430pm). Amenities were clean, site was clean-ish (it’s sand site).

Limitations were:
- not enough grass area for kids to run around
- camp kitchen had uneven ground; a few kids during our stay has tripped over the ground
- not enough activities for kids (a swimming pool will be a lovely addition)

Stayed at Margaret River Discovery parks for the christmas /new years period and it went very well.

Located really close to the middle of the Margaret River regions, it is very close to few wineries, couple minutes away to the town, and you are able to stargaze in the dark.

This place is very clean, and provided everything you needed. This place also have some animals /pets which make you feels closer to the nature.

On top of that, it is in a very reasonable price.

Smack bang in the middle of Margaret River, Discovery Parks MR hosts big sites, well established trees providing shelter and a beautiful farm backdrop. The facilities are consistently well maintained but could do with a mat in the showers to have something to stand on when exiting the shower. This campsite is extremely family friendly and hosts animal feedings at 4:30 to 5:00 which provide a wonderful experience for visiting families. Would recommend for your trip to Margaret River.

Stayed here over the New Year period, we camped and our friends stayed in the cabins. Both the cabins and the park ammenities were extremely clean and smelt nice. The camp kitchen and BBQ area was also lovely. Live music was organised for NYE, which was free for all to attend. Staff were friendly and helpful, but not over the top with park rules. Great location to wineries and breweries for during the day. Plenty of shade. Definitely recommend staying and we will be back! Well done to all.
We had a wonderful time.

Formerly known as Tauntons farm stay, this is now a Discovery park. Signage roadside still says Tauntons and Apple maps takes you somewhere else, google Maps takes you right there. Staff are aware of the problem with signage and Apple maps.
Cabin was 2 bed self contained ,1 room with a double bed and 1 room with 4 bunks. Beds are comfortable and lovely crisp white sheets. Basic kitchen gas hob no oven. There are lots of ducks coming onto the balcony. Very quite at night . I'm definitely going to go back. Little play area for kids with giant bouncy pillow. You can feed the animals at 430pm.
Only negative is the verandas have no rails so if you have small kids it's a fall risk.

Great sites .
Nice park.

3 night, pre booked stay, 2 adults, WA school holidays last weekend. October 4-6th. Park is situated just  north of Cowaramup on the highway, with a dedicated turn lane. We took issue however as the signage had not been updated to reflect Discovery Park and instead read Taunton Family/Big 4 leaving us to think we had the wrong park and went in into town to call and check. It would have been helpful to have advised us by email /call or text of this when we booked. We raised this at reception when we checked in and was told signage was delayed, this was the same response when I emailed head office to advise that the issue had caused us a near accident when trying to re-enter the highway after we thought it was the wrong place.
The park itself is rustic, borders in part onto a dam with terrific birdlife and is geared towards families with a big play area, daily animal feeding and lots of green space. Big camp kitchen and well appointed although dated amenities are well kept. We had a large site which was facing the dam but all others were generous sizing and plenty of aspects to choose from.  Daily duck family visits were lovely and although the park was busy it was a great few days.

We have been coming here for years with great service. Unfortunately since the change of owners it has gone the wrong direction. The reception staff have very little care for anything you bring up. You never used to be allowed to bring dogs during school holidays, but that had recently changed. Now I feel like I'm living next to to a dog shelter. My partner fell through a concrete slab and not a word from reception or management.
This place used to be good.

We enjoyed our stay, it’s definitely a great spot for a family getaway. ||We booked a two bedroom chalet (10) - main bedroom had a queen bed and other room had bunks. We managed to squeeze a portacot in the bunk room.|||Air/heating via split system |Ample blankets and towels provided |Separate toilet from the bathroom|No in-chalet laundry facility but the main utility block is located at the bottom of the hill|No balustrade around the deck/balcony |Basic accommodation overall but clean and comfortable|Largish grassed area outside our chalet which was great for the kids||The kids loved the animal feeding and play ground are||All the staff we met were lovely and friendly ||We will definitely stay again
Clean

Fantastic weekend away, we loved this park and will definitely return. There was so much to do, from animal feeding, jumping pillow, playground, peddle carts and so close to great wineries and attractions. The Cabin was petfect for a small family, basic kitchen and bathroom, comfortable and clean. The kitchen had a stove top and microwave plus a full sized fridge, kettle and toaster. Basics were provided including some tea/coffee, milk, salt and pepper. The reception sells a few basics and on a hot afternoon we bought some icecreams.

What a fantastic park. Neat n tidy and clean amenities. Large sites. Staff are very friendly and helpful.
There's is a great camp kitchen with potbelly fire for the cooler nights. The children love feeding the friendly camel, pig, sheep, cows n goat. Close to Cowaramup and all the wineries. A 15 minute drive to Margaret River. The park is quiet, a great spot to base ourselves for a couple of weeks while we discover all the wider area has to offer.
The park is dog friendly.

Was an awesome stay.lovely service  Clean facilities, clean park plenty of room to play, awesome scenery and the camp kitchen was the best I’ve seen with the added bonus of a fire pit that can be lit with wood supplied by the park. Will be coming back for sure.

Such a pretty location, great feel, farm animals are fed daily
Staff are excellent,  rooms very clean
Loved my stay.
Lovely place to stay. Lots to do for kids. Very close to Cowaramup town. Rooms very neat and clean. So spacious rooms.

FANTASTIC place! We arrived at night and can't really see the surrounding as its quite dark. In the morning when we open up our door, the scenery is wonderful. There is even flock of ducks on our doorstep. They even asked if our tea is enough and offer to top it up.

Taunton Farm was loads of fun.  The kids enjoyed feeding the animals daily and bouncing on the huge pillow.  We were welcomed warmly and it took us less than 5min to check-in.   We stayed in a cottage and although it was a wee bit small, it was nice and cosy for a family of 2 adults and 2 young kids.  We were visited by a couple of ducks that walked right up to our doorstep which was a real treat.  Supermarkets and restaurants are a short drive away making this a nice base to visit the rest of Margaret River area||Michael LeCaine |Singapore, Jun 2024

Great location as always,  love the kitchen area with fireplace and firepit.
Good clean facilities,  only complaint is its the second time we have had our camp site totally flooded by rain. Think sites need to be raised and or upgraded.
Otherwise great friendly location.
Great place to base yourself for touring the north Margaret River Region.
Its defenatly for families with children. Daily animal feeding.

Located just north of Cowaramup, the caravan park is well located for visiting the Margaret River area and especially the northern sections.||Just off the road, it is a reasonably quiet location.  Reception staff are both friendly and helpful.  The park is both well presented and nicely laid out with large sites and good amenities.  There is a daily animal feeding which is enjoyed by all.  Mobile phone coverage is good.

Great for kids to keep busy
Terrific camp kitchen
The sandy bays make it a very dirty cleanup after yr stay tho

Our recent stay at Taunton Farm Holiday Park provided a mixed bag of experiences worth noting.

Upon arrival, we were greeted warmly by the staff, who unfortunately couldn’t accommodate our request to swap our uneven tent site for a flatter ground due to full booking. This left us grappling with a tent site littered with tree roots, making it uncomfortable to navigate and raising concerns about potential damage to our gear.

Despite this setback, the inclusion of ensuite facilities was a welcome convenience, albeit with occasional wait times due to limited units. An expansion of these facilities could greatly enhance the overall guest experience.

On the downside, the outdated kitchen facilities left much to be desired, indicating the need for significant upgrades to align with contemporary standards.

However, the campground’s proximity to Margaret River’s tourist attractions was a definite plus, offering easy access to local sights and activities.

In conclusion, while Taunton Farm Holiday Park has its merits, including friendly staff and convenient amenities, the uneven tent sites and outdated kitchen facilities detracted from an otherwise enjoyable stay. We hope that improvements will be made to address these concerns and elevate the guest experience in the future.
Awesome place for families

Love staying at Taunton.  Sites are big & plenty of shade. We have stayed many times in the last month while waiting for appointments & each time was as good as the last. Big shout out to the cleaners
absolutely spotless & maintaince were on top of everything. The country outlook is so relaxing.  Special thanks to Anne for all her help & all other staff who were just great. Hopefully be back on our return trip.

It was our first time camping with our newly renovated caravan the site was well shaded slight gradient and due to low rainfall little grass so the park was offering ground sheet for the awning area.|Very friendly and helpful staff plus great fun when it was feeding time for the pet farm animals, very funny seeing city kids experience farm animals for the first time.|Very clean and well maintained facilities, nice hot showers.
Lovely staff, great location. But, bay had a fair slope to it, also a lot of leave plus very sandy. Toilets and showers nice and clean. Good price.

Nice place to visit. Quiet nights and nice easy, relaxing place to be. Plenty of shade for caravans etc. Lots of cabins to stay in too. Only problem was the bird poop all over the car but easily cleaned. Typical bush camp ground with sand/ gravel site pads. Very clean ablutions & showers. Dog friendly.
Great park, daughter loved it great facility's
Does not work to book online and they don’t answer the phone on open hours👎🏼

We don't normally leave reviews however after seeing the negative feedback from a group staying at the same time as us over the long weekend we wanted to provide feedback. This park is just lovely and the staff amazing. When we used the amenities they were spotless and a credit to the cleaners. Yes the sites are dry and given the region hasn't had rain for several months and the park on dam water the staff do a wonderful job of keeping common areas green. Brilliant location central to all that the region has. Sites are huge in comparison to many parks we have visited. We will definitely be back.

My wife and I have been staying here for the past 15 years or so,we love it.Anne and Steve are both very friendly  polite and professional.They are both hard working and keep the park clean and tidy.So we will definitely be coming here for the next 15 years.

We booked a 1 bedroom cabin  for 2 nights and attended a wedding close by.  The cabins are enormous with high ceilings, large bathroom and queen bed  plus double bunks . Also air-conditioned  TV and big fridge. |Staff are very nice and the animal feeding is great to see at 5pm daily.|10mins to Margaret River and closer to Cowaramup. |Comfort and everything you need.

Really disappointed with Big4U name here...The powered sites we stayed were all sandy and dusty, no grass at all opposite to what is advertised. Also the kitchen was not clean and not enuff cooking and dish washing spots there, one of the two the bbq hotplate was not working so had to cancel our bbq meal because of rush, kitchen and toilets were messy all the time and not cleaned frequently

Unfortunately a little unpleasant experience during the stay.
Positives are:
-	Central location and close to Margaret River attractions,
-	Farm experience,
-	Animal feeding,
-	Giant pillow is enjoyable.
Although it is a member of the Big 4, it is not up to that mark with certain services and amenities.
Negatives are:
-	Dusty and uneven sites vs grassy sites shown on park website photos.
-	Limited amenities and services, particularly to cater when large groups arrivals. Excuses of busy/ peak holiday guests arrival are not reasonable when guests have spent money to stay.
-	Closing and locking services for guests in not a resolution to the problem. This must have been discussed or prompted to the guests.
-	BBQ Hot plate not working during the stay, which took more time waiting in a queue and the time around kitchen area for food prep.

Great location and park ONLY if they manage properly. Website shows grass to camping sites but in actual it's only DUST. Amenities were not cleaned and not enough. Only one sink for washing dishes. Similarly only one hot plate was working out of two.
Lady on reception was very rude.

Would not recommend until they improve

Location was good that's why we booked it but we found services were below average.Cleanliness was not as per standard.Amenities were not enough as per number of accomodations.Dont recommend till they improve.
Pathetic experience

We booked 7 blue gum sites for 3 night and 4 days for our large group of 7 families. None of the site has any traces of grass, it was just the dust floor with heaps of leaves around everywhere but the photo on the park website is showing a grassed area.

Services was just so so, bathroom cleaning services were very bad.
One of new ablution block was locked by management on 3rd day of our stay and reopened on our last day at the checkout time.

The old ablution block always remain out of towel tissue paper.

There were two BBQ plate but one BBQ plate was not functioning.

Kitchen flour is not paved or tiled. It is just the sand floor. Our family also witnessed a dead mouse behind the oven in the kitchen.
Only one kitchen sink and the size of kitchen sink is not suitable for facilitating large number of park guests.

We can't believe that Big4 holiday park will be like this. Not recommended untill they improve their sites and the services.

However, The Kids loves the bouncing pillow.
Location of farm is fantastic, easy access to beaches and other tourist spot. The site is surrounded by beautiful landscape of the farms.
This place was wonderful we stayed in a cabin and my son loved the animal feeding at 5pm especially Mickey the pig.. he loves pigs

Very disappointed as never thought BIG4 park would be like this. Found dead mice in camping kitchen..
It's not even a farm.. very little amenities especially lacking toilets and kitchen amenities
Jumping Pillow needs shade as got very hot and wish they had a pool

What a fantastic family stay! We had booked a powered tent site for 3 nights 4 days - wishing we had booked longer. It was close to town, beach’s and other tourist attractions. The staff were very friendly and helpful, the children helped feed the animals at 5pm daily and we were treated to a movie night!

Thank you, we will be back!
Can't thank Anne enough for getting us in last minute when we were booked in else where the day after! Can't thank the team enough.

We were so lucky to find Taunton Farm as the perfect setting for our ‘destination’ wedding venue. We had family coming from a far and wanted to celebrate a full weekend with our nearest and dearest. Taunton created this without a fuss. We booked the full venue for the weekend, It was affordable and let us create exactly what we wanted for our special day!
The grounds were immaculate and our guests that stayed were welcomed and comfortable.
We absolutely cannot thank the team enough for providing the most amazing service with everything that a wedding entails! (Anne and Steve were incredible, they had fantastic communication in the lead up to the wedding and for the full duration of the weekend. We couldn’t have done it without them!)
It feels like family at Taunton ❤️
Really nice place to camp, nice location with everything close by, helpful and friendly staff in reception, toilets and showers always spotless, will definitely go back again

I have been coming to Taunton Farm for nearly 30 years in the middle of January for a Square dance that has been held in initially Margaret River and now Cowarmup, Taunton Farm used to be a beautiful grassy area but now is  total DUST bowl, I definitely will not be staying there again 😞

Location a bit far from town. The facilities were ok, some sites very small. The staff was friendly, just one lady a bit rude and not flexible for a late departure of 2 hour (the camping wasn't even busy)

We camped 4 nights at BIG4 Taunton Farm Margaret River…off the highway; great staff; super family friendly; peak times such as January, is at full capacity; farm animals; an occasion camel ride; kids have the joy of a large jumping pillow; facilities ok; not a lot of protection from the wind with AM and PM regular 40km wind gusts coming through (great for drying your hair in super duper quick time); drive to all locations you want to visit; camping enjoyable but you will be on top of your neighbours so if you want a national park experience with some space and nature beyond a few trees this is not the place for you, for us it was perfect as we just wanted to get out and explore WA. This is your perfect base camp if you plan to be out and about.
Nice welcoming, shaded, hygienic place.

Simply the best location completely relaxing wonderful staff and so helpful
Spotlessly clean and really safe environment close to everything MR has to offer.

Fabulous 2nd stay in December 2023 this time we joined a group of 50 friends. Everyone had a great stay on enquiry the chalets were comfortable and clean. Our site was shady and close to ablutions. Rustic Camp kitchen clean barbecues and our group had a great few nights using the old school rooms for functions. Thank you to the staff who went visibly working hard to keep up a good venue. Farm animals were in view most of the day.
We were caravanning. Sites good and facilities good. View pleasant over the farm. Flies unpleasant but that is bush camping

This is a beautiful caravan park surrounded by farm country. There are many lovely trees in the park, the sites are quite large, and the amenities were clean and relatively modern. The park is suitable for families with young children, and also for older travellers who like tranquiliy like us, because there are areas of the park well away from the playground and jumping pillow. The reception staff were friendly, and gave helpful suggestions regarding activities in the area.  We'd happily stay here again.

We had a large family group for a chrissy get together. Some in tents and caravans and chalets. I stayed in a chalet. Lovely and very well appointed. Clean and the linen was pristine. Had 2 very comfy nights. We all thoroughly enjoyed our stay. Pizza van and coffee van was amazing too. Thanks again we will definitely be back

We loved our stay at Taunton Farm! Customer service was consistent from the time of booking to check out, staff were brilliant- from front counter to caretakers they were always smiling and very helpful.
Plenty for the kids to do, with pedal bikes, playground, jumping pillow. Loved the little shop in the office for extra supplies - ice creams and baby sunscreen.
Perfect location - we managed to visit the town centre, various breweries, wineries, attractions and beaches in our short stay.
We had a drive through caravan site and after setting up there was still ample of room left - (very generous sites!)
Highly recommend and looking forward to returning. 😃
Great stay. They did everything to extend our booking due to car issues. Terrific staff. Great activities for kids. Lots of shade. Excellent amenities. Difficult to fault.

What a wonderful place for a family getaway
We Camped it was rainy few days  but that was OK
Campers kitchen is amazing with wood fire plenty of room
Clean  free bbqs
Nice hot free flowing showers ablution blocks clean
Feed the animals was the highlight of the day for children
Staff are welcoming , friendly happy to chat
Nicest Park we have  ever to ...
We will be back .

Thank you 😊
Great location. Near farms and wineries. Friendly staff. Animal feeding was fun for child. Clean and comfortable cottage with heated bed. (Just one side was heated though)
Love this place. I’ve been coming for over 20 years and it’s simply beautiful

Well located just north of Margaret River. St. Stayed 2 nights and found it very relaxing. Great sites, amenities and booking service. Very peaceful. If you have young people with you, they will be able to get into feeding the animals. Recommended. Take the opportunity to go next door to the cheese 🧀 etc facility. Good stuff!!!
